a:r.a:t: Three supporting subsystems handle data persistence, monitoring, and quality assurance. Persistence is built on PostgreSQL with the pgvector extension for storing and searching vector embeddings. Session transcripts, job records and results, and all tenant and user data are stored in Postgres with proper data isolation between tenants. The system supports database migrations for schema evolution. Observability integrates with LangFuse, an open-source LLM observability platform, for distributed tracing. Every tool call emits events that are captured as spans in a trace. Router decisions are logged for analysis. Turn-level status tracking provides real-time visibility into what the system is doing. SSE progress streaming pushes live updates to the frontend so users see agent progress in real time. An events.jsonl audit trail captures admin actions for compliance. The benchmark suite provides automated quality assurance. It includes a defense RAG test corpus with 245 lines of benchmark infrastructure for testing retrieval accuracy. Adversarial prompt testing validates the system against injection attacks. Regression detection catches silent quality degradation -- the kind of bug where the system still works but answers get subtly worse. Latency profiling measures performance per tool, and token cost analysis tracks LLM spending. The Docker sandbox for code execution uses 180-second timeouts in isolated containers. The control panel frontend is a separate React and TypeScript application built with Vite.
